name: ydata-synthetic
tagline: Generate synthetic tabular and time-series data with GANs and Gaussian Mixtures
metaDescription: ydata-synthetic generates synthetic tabular and time-series data using models like CTGAN, TimeGAN, and Gaussian Mixtures, with a Python API and Streamlit UI.
overview: ydata-synthetic is a Python package for generating synthetic tabular and time-series data from real datasets. It bundles several generative models, from Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to a faster Gaussian Mixture approach, so you can produce artificial data that keeps the statistical shape of the original without carrying identifiable records.

It is aimed at data scientists and ML engineers who need extra or privacy-safe data. Common reasons include sharing data without exposing personal information, balancing or augmenting small datasets, and reducing bias before training a model.

In the synthetic data category, it covers both structured tables and sequential data. The deep learning models are built on TensorFlow 2, and a Streamlit UI is included so you can train a synthesizer and generate samples without writing much code. Note: the project was renamed and now installs from the fg-data-synthetic package.
features: Generates both tabular and time-series synthetic data from real datasets
Includes multiple GAN models: CGAN, WGAN, WGANGP, DRAGAN, CRAMER, and CTGAN
Supports time-series generation with TimeGAN and DoppelGANger
Offers a fast Gaussian Mixture model that runs without a GPU
Ships a Streamlit UI to train a synthesizer and generate or profile samples
Deep learning models implemented on TensorFlow 2 with example notebooks

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package from PyPI, then either use the Python API or launch the Streamlit UI. Note that the package is now published as fg-data-synthetic and imported as data_synthetic.

steps.heading: Install the package
steps.body: Install the latest release from PyPI with pip.

steps.heading: Import a synthesizer
steps.body: Import the regular synthesizer from the data_synthetic package to generate tabular data.

steps.heading: Optional: install and run the Streamlit UI
steps.body: For a low-code experience, install the streamlit extra and start the app from a Python file (notebooks are not supported).
steps.code:
pip install fg-data-synthetic[streamlit]

# in a python file:
from data_synthetic import streamlit_app
streamlit_app.run()

useCases: Share a dataset for analysis or ML without exposing personal or identifiable records
Augment a small training set with additional synthetic rows to improve model coverage
Balance an imbalanced dataset by generating more samples of underrepresented classes
Create realistic synthetic time-series, such as stock or usage data, for testing and prototyping
